SEARCH

Package com.nib.commons.io.vsam

package com.nib.commons.io.vsam
Provides classes and interfaces for emulating VSAM (Virtual Storage Access Method) file organizations.

This package includes implementations for different types of VSAM datasets:

  • EntrySequentialFile - Entry-Sequenced Data Set (ESDS)
  • KeySequentialFile - Key-Sequenced Data Set (KSDS)
  • RecordRelativeFile - Relative-Record Data Set (RRDS)

It also provides abstraction layers for I/O operations, generic SQL-based storage backends, and builder classes for configuring VSAM file access.

  • Related Packages
    Package
    Description
    com.nib.commons.io
    Provides core Input/Output (I/O) components and abstractions for handling various file organizations and access methods.
    com.nib.commons.io.cache
    Provides caching mechanisms for I/O operations.
    com.nib.commons.io.catalog
    Provides catalog management mechanisms for I/O operations.
    com.nib.commons.io.exceptions
    Provides exception classes for I/O operations.
    com.nib.commons.io.tools
    Provides command-line tools and utilities for file system operations.
  • Class
    Description
    AbstractEntrySequentialFile
    Abstract class representing an entry-sequential VSAM (Virtual Storage Access Method) file.
    AbstractIOClass
     
    AbstractKeySequentialFile
    Represents an abstract base class for Key-Sequenced Data Set (KSDS) and Alternate Index (AIX) file handling within a VSAM (Virtual Storage Access Method) system.
    AbstractKeySequentialFile.Key
     
    AbstractRecordRelativeFile
    Represents an abstract base class for handling relative record datasets (RRDS) in a VSAM (Virtual Storage Access Method) file organization.
    AbstractSqlApi
     
    AbstractVsamFile
    AbstractVsamFile serves as a base class for implementing various types of VSAM (Virtual Storage Access Method) file operations.
    Db2SqlApi
     
    DuplicateKeyFoundException
    Exception indicating that a key is duplicated in the dataset.
    EntrySequentialFile
    Represents a sequential file for managing file I/O operations within a specific context or database connection.
    H2SqlApi
     
    IllegalRecordLengthException
    Exception indicating that record length is not correct.
    InvalidVsamOrganizationException
    Exception indicating that the requested VSAM organization doesn't match the actual dataset organization.
    IOOperation
     
    IVsamApi
    Interface representing the API for interacting with VSAM (Virtual Storage Access Method) files.
    KeyIs
    Represents types of key operations in a Key Sequenced Data Set (KSDS) context.
    KeyNotFoundException
    Exception indicating that a key was not found during file operations.
    KeySequentialFile
    Represents a key-sequential file object, facilitating operations such as reading, writing, and modifying records within the file.
    KsdsKey
    Represents a key in a Key Sequenced Data Set (KSDS) in a VSAM environment.
    MsSqlApi
     
    MySqlApi
     
    PgSqlApi
     
    Rba
    Represents a Relative Buffer Address (RBA), which is a long value used to denote the position of a record within a file relative to the start of the file's buffer.
    RecordRelativeFile
    The RecordRelativeFile class extends the functionality of AbstractRecordRelativeFile and provides methods to handle relative record file operations, including reading, writing, rewriting, and deleting records.
    Rrn
    Represents a Relative Record Number (RRN), which is a long value used to denote the position of a record within a file relative to the start of the file.
    TableAlreadyExistsException
    Exception indicating that the corresponding already exists
    TableNotFoundException
    Exception indicating that the corresponding table was not found.
    VsamApiBuilder
    Factory class for creating instances of IVsamApi and ICatalogApi based on the provided AbstractFile and DatabaseConnection.
    VsamEmptyException
    Exception indicating that the corresponding table was not found.
    VsamOrganization
    Enumeration representing different VSAM (Virtual Storage Access Method) organizations.
